prompt: In a short public memo, Argument: "Late-shift commute complaints improved after extend evening bus service. Therefore, the action caused the improvement." What is the logical gap?

choices.text: It gives too many definitions of the same term.
It states the conclusion before the evidence.
It uses a source with no possible incentive.
It assumes timing alone proves causation.

explanation: An event happening first does not by itself prove it caused the later change.

prompt: In a short public memo, Argument: "Most surveyed ninth-grade teachers liked require phone lockers during class. Therefore, every person affected will benefit from it." What is the logical gap?

choices.text: It uses a source with no possible incentive.
It moves from what most surveyed people liked to what everyone will benefit from.
It states the conclusion before the evidence.
It gives too many definitions of the same term.

explanation: Preference among most respondents does not prove universal benefit.

prompt: In a short public memo, Argument: "The average result improved after send medication reminder texts. Therefore, each individual patients over 6 improved." What is the logical gap?

choices.text: It states the conclusion before the evidence.
It treats an average change as proof that every individual changed the same way.
It uses a source with no possible incentive.
It gives too many definitions of the same term.

explanation: Averages can rise even when some individuals do not improve.

It should be ignored because every signal has some errors.
It proves the base rate no longer matters.
It proves the flagged problem is definitely present.

explanation: Probabilistic claims express degrees of uncertainty, not guarantees.

prompt: In a short public memo, In one city transit comparison, Group A had 5 cases out of 100. Group B had 7 cases out of 300. Which group had the higher rate?

choices.text: The rates are automatically equal because both groups are in the same study.
Group A, because 5/100 is a higher rate than 7/300.
Group B, because 300 is larger than 100.
Group A, because 5 is smaller than 7.

explanation: Statistical comparisons require attention to denominators and the difference between percentage points and relative percent change.

choices.text: Become completely certain the action works in every setting.
Ignore the evidence because no single item can matter at all.
Reverse your view even if the new evidence supports your prior view.
Increase confidence somewhat, while staying open to better evidence.

explanation: Belief changes should be proportional to the strength and limits of the evidence.
